title: A Song for the Schedule
description: Busker attempts to compose for Brasston. Alchemist critiques. Comedic character beat.

steps.text: The Busker had been sitting on a crate for ninety minutes, scoring a piece for Brasston. It was not going well.

lines.speaker: Busker
lines.text: Any of them. All of them. Brasston isn't in 4/4 and it isn't in 3/4. It's in 47/38.

lines.speaker: Alchemist
lines.text: That is not a time signature.

lines.speaker: Busker
lines.text: Tell that to the clock tower.

lines.speaker: Busker
lines.text: Thank you. The bridge is in 13/11 and I cannot, for the life of me, make it resolve.

lines.speaker: Busker
lines.text: You know, that's the most useful thing you've ever said.

lines.speaker: Busker
lines.text: I'll credit you in the liner notes.

steps.text: The Busker scored the final bar in silence. In Brasston, silence was itself a rhythm. The piece would not resolve. The piece was not supposed to.
